title: The Gang Finds a Dead Guy

jokes.text: I'm not talking about killing the guy. I'm talking about going with a group... and intimidate him, maybe break his arm. No more Super Bowls for that pretty boy.
jokes.setup: Casual discussion about intimidating someone
jokes.punchline: Revealing the target is Tom Brady and the delusion of actually threatening him
jokes.explanation: Sets up Mac's delusional confidence early - the absurdity of threatening Tom Brady establishes character perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm not talking about killing the guy. I'm talking about going with a group... and intimidate him, maybe break his arm. No more Super Bowls for that pretty boy.

jokes.text: Tom Brady would kick your ass.
jokes.setup: Mac's ridiculous threat against Tom Brady
jokes.punchline: Dennis stating the obvious with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to Mac's delusion, economical and character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Tom Brady would kick your ass.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. He shit his pants, Dee.
jokes.setup: Finding an unconscious old man in the bar
jokes.punchline: The gross reality of the situation bluntly stated

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, my God. He shit his pants, Dee.

dialogue.line: Oh, I don't wanna know that.

jokes.text: I'm gonna poke him with this. Get up, old man. This isn't the American Legion.

jokes.punchline: The casual cruelty and specific reference to American Legion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's callousness with a specific, quotable insult

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm gonna poke him with this. Get up, old man. This isn't the American Legion.

jokes.text: Holy shit. That bitch is dead.

jokes.punchline: Casual, almost annoyed realization that he's dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy delivery - the casual callousness and blunt language is both shocking and hilarious

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Holy shit. That bitch is dead.

jokes.text: This ought to do wonders for business.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: This ought to do wonders for business.

dialogue.line: We're not gonna burn it.

jokes.explanation: Standard Dennis vanity response, predictable but in character

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Out of my league? She's out of your league. I could get that chick.

jokes.text: Pop-Pop is an asshole, Sweet Dee. And I'm placing my bets that that girl's grandfather is an asshole too.
jokes.setup: Dee expressing sympathy about abandoned elderly
jokes.punchline: Dennis's cynical assumption that all abandoned elderly deserved it
jokes.explanation: Dennis's casual cruelty toward family and cynical worldview perfectly characterized, and sets up the Pop-Pop revelation

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Pop-Pop is an asshole, Sweet Dee. And I'm placing my bets that that girl's grandfather is an asshole too.

jokes.text: And I'm placing my bets that that girl's grandfather is an asshole too. People don't just abandon their parents for no good reason.
jokes.setup: Discussion about abandoned elderly
jokes.punchline: Dennis's cynical worldview presented as wisdom
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with dramatic irony - we'll learn he's right about the grandfather being terrible, but for reasons Dennis can't imagine.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: And I'm placing my bets that that girl's grandfather is an asshole too. People don't just abandon their parents for no good reason.

jokes.text: Dude, have you ever been to a nursing home? People abandon their parents all the time. It's a sad, throwaway culture we live in.
jokes.setup: Dennis dismissing abandoned elderly
jokes.punchline: Mac suddenly becoming philosophical about societal problems
jokes.explanation: Mac unexpectedly showing insight, which is funny because it's so out of character for him

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dude, have you ever been to a nursing home? People abandon their parents all the time. It's a sad, throwaway culture we live in.

jokes.text: Whoa. Geez, your place is looking... good.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Whoa. Geez, your place is looking... good.

jokes.text: Sounds a little mean, but I find them kind of creepy and scary and gross. I think they're gross. It's their hands mostly. You know, how you can see right through them.
jokes.setup: Dee explaining why she needs Charlie to come with her
jokes.punchline: Her honest but horrible description of elderly people
jokes.explanation: Dee's shallow vanity leading to genuine revulsion at aging is perfectly in character and cringe-funny

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Sounds a little mean, but I find them kind of creepy and scary and gross. I think they're gross. It's their hands mostly. You know, how you can see right through them.

jokes.text: It was Christmas Eve last year and, uh... I was at Lionel's house, making him dinner. And we got to talking about the spirit of giving... So we did, we went down there. And not only did we feed... every last poor, miserable, precious soul... but we washed the feet of every man, woman and child there... in the spirit of Christ.

jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate and religious lies about their relationship
jokes.explanation: Dennis's elaborate fiction escalating to Christ-like imagery is masterful manipulation and perfectly in character

jokes.text: Okay. I find this offensive for so many reasons. I'm guessing you acquired this through illegal means. For me to take this from you would be an extension of that. And secondly, that you would expect it would 'make my day'... assumes that I'm interested in profiting off the murder of millions of innocent people.
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie trying to sell Nazi uniform to museum
jokes.punchline: The curator's moral outrage at their casual approach to Nazi memorabilia
jokes.explanation: Good reality check showing how normal people react to the gang's amoral behavior

dialogue.line: Okay. I find this offensive for so many reasons. I'm guessing you acquired this through illegal means. For me to take this from you would be an extension of that. And secondly, that you would expect it would 'make my day'... assumes that I'm interested in profiting off the murder of millions of innocent people.

jokes.text: How much will you give us for it? Nothing. Nothing, or... I plan to call the police the minute you guys leave my office.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: How much will you give us for it?

dialogue.line: Nothing. Nothing, or... I plan to call the police the minute you guys leave my office.
